seoTitle: Wembley Downs vs West Beach — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Wembley Downs and West Beach. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Wembley Downs vs West Beach: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wembley Downs and West Beach.
heading: Wembley Downs vs West Beach — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wembley Downs, Western Australia and West Beach, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wembley Downs has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to West Beach (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wembley Downs or West Beach safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wembley Downs has a flood risk score of 23/100, while West Beach scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wembley Downs scores 23/100 for bushfire risk. West Beach scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wembley Downs or West Beach?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wembley Downs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
